Question: Let $$\mathbf{\text{G}} ( x , y ) = x^{2} y \mathbf{\text{i}} - ( x + y ) \mathbf{\text{j}}$$ be a vector field in $$\RR^{2} .$$ What vector is associated with the point $$( -2 , 3 ) ?$$
Draw the radial field $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y ) = - \frac{x}{3} \mathbf{\text{i}} - \frac{y}{3} \mathbf{\text{j}} .$$
Sketch vector field $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y ) = \langle -2 y , 2 x \rangle .$$ Is the vector field radial, rotational, or neither?

Vector field $$v ( x , y ) = \langle 4 | x | , 1 \rangle$$ models the velocity of water on the surface of a river. What is the speed of the water at point $$( 2 , 3 ) ?$$ Use meters per second as the units.
Is vector field $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y ) = \langle \text{unicode[:-]} y , x \rangle$$ a unit vector field?
Sketch vector field $$\mathbf{\text{G}} ( x , y , z ) = \langle 2 , \frac{z}{2} , 1 \rangle .$$

The mass of asteroid 1 is 750,000 kg and the mass of asteroid 2 is 130,000 kg. Assume asteroid 1 is located at the origin, and asteroid 2 is located at $$( 15 , -5 , 10 ) ,$$ measured in units of 10 to the eighth power kilometers. Given that the universal gravitational constant is $$G = 6.67384 \times 10^{-11} \text{m}^{3} \text{kg}^{-1} \text{s}^{-2} ,$$ find the gravitational force vector that asteroid 1 exerts on asteroid 2.
Use technology to plot the gradient vector field of $$f ( x , y ) = \text{sin} x \text{cos} y .$$

Is $$f ( x , y , z ) = x^{2} \text{cos} ( y z ) + y^{2} z^{2}$$ a potential function for $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y , z ) = \langle 2 x \text{cos} ( y z ) , \text{unicode[:-]} x^{2} z \text{sin} ( y z ) + 2 y z^{2} , y^{2} \rangle ?$$
Verify that $$f ( x , y ) = x^{3} y^{2} + x$$ is a potential function for velocity field $$\textbf{v} \left(\right. x , y \left.\right) = \langle 3 x^{2} y^{2} , 2 x^{3} y \rangle .$$
Show that the vector field $$\mathbf{\text{F}} ( x , y ) = y \mathbf{\text{i}} - x^{2} x y \mathbf{\text{j}}$$ is not conservative.
Is vector field $$G ( x , y , z ) = \langle y , x , x y z \rangle$$ conservative?
